mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single quarter, labor market deterioration triggers recession fears that compress equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity, which overrides monetary easing benefits and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 100 and HOUST falls below 1400
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with Rank IC > 0.05 when tested against single-series baselines over a 5-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 3.5%, aggressive federal funds rate hikes fail to anchor inflation expectations, causing equity multiples to compress and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL exceeds 305 and FEDFUNDS rises above 5.25%
failure_condition: Forward returns in high-inflation/hiking periods do not significantly underperform baseline (p > 0.05), or the signal produces positive IC in > 40% of out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates expanding corporate profit margins and revenue growth, which mechanically supports equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative or near zero over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical difference from zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls fall below 157000 for two consecutive months, labor market cooling accelerates into broader demand destruction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls below 157000 for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered windows, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling real consumer spending signals supply-demand imbalance that compresses corporate margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above 102 and RSAFS falls below 700000
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve model fit (AIC/BIC) over additive single-series models, or forward returns show no directional bias in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: During periods where the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, a rising unemployment rate accelerates equity risk repricing, producing strongly negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as discount rates and recession probabilities spike simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0% and UNRATE rises above 3.8%
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with labor softening
failure_condition: Forward returns in restrictive/high-unemployment regimes do not significantly underperform baseline (p > 0.05), or the signal produces positive IC in > 40% of out-of-sample windows.
